repo_state.notes: Workspace already had unrelated untracked files under code/option3-overlap/ and findings/artifacts/option-3/.

metrics.key_question: Does native equality/rebuild invalidation preserve semantics on rebuild-heavy regressions?
metrics.option_implication: Native rebuild semantics are sound on these cases, but DD integration needs explicit row-level invalidation counters.
metrics.performance_summary: Three equality/rebuild checks passed in 1440569 us total.

runs.notes: Direct egglog run exited 0 with one non-strict-mode warning.

limitations: Current binaries do not expose counters for canonical-id rewrites, rebuild-index hits, row retractions, row reinserts, or refresh-row counts.
The runtime runs confirm semantic pass/fail only; the fine-grained row counts are source-inferred, not instrumented.

verdict: Supported by source and runtime checks; equality/rebuild invalidation is functioning, but row-level counters are not surfaced by the current binaries.
